title: selago karooica
intro: Selago karooica, also known as the Karroo Blue or Blue Carpet, is a low-growing, evergreen perennial plant native to South Africa. It belongs to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) and is prized for its vibrant blue flowers and potential medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, Selago karooica is often used to create teas, infusions, and extracts that are said to promote relaxation, reduce pain, and support overall well-being.
